completed_work: Extended prompt_context build_latest_context_pack_manifest and build_latest_context_pack_text with explicit L0 passthrough.
Relaxed ask_provider.py and compare_providers.py --include-l0 validation so it works with --latest-context prompt generation.
Added provider CLI tests proving explicit L0 IDs reach latest-context prompt builders.
key_decisions: Treat --include-l0 as a latest-context provider prompt feature, not only a JSON context-summary preview feature.
Preserve backward compatibility by adding include_l0/l0_store kwargs only when explicit L0 IDs are supplied.
next_actions: Record this memory refresh, then decide whether the next target is explicit L0 exclusion diagnostics in provider dry runs or automatic L0 discovery from selected artifacts.
risks: Automatic L0 retrieval/ranking is still not implemented; this remains an explicit user-seeded path.
summary_text: Explicit L0 context-pack inclusion is now wired into provider prompt generation. Previously --include-l0 was limited to JSON context-summary preview. The provider prompt helpers now accept include_l0 and l0_store, and ask_provider.py / compare_providers.py pass those values into latest-context construction when explicit L0 IDs are requested. The CLI guard now requires --include-l0 to be used with --latest-context, while retaining the JSON-only requirement when --include-l0 is combined with --print-context-summary. Existing call sites remain compatible because include_l0 and l0_store are only passed through when requested. Focused provider tests and the full suite passed before commit.
